title: Strategist, Agent Development
requirements_met: Product strategy + customer insight: led GTM, positioning, messaging at Minima; user-facing product marketing
Coordinate technical + non-technical stakeholders: worked across dev, marketing, ops, founders for 6 yrs
Outstanding communication: PR (Bloomberg/Yahoo, 2M views), 40K X following, keynotes
Drive execution of complex multi-workstream projects: ran presale ($4.5M), ambassador programme (75K nodes)
Direct exposure to building AI agents: currently building agents with Claude Code (conversational/agentic)
gaps: Strong software-development understanding expected; Ryan codes (Python/TS) but is not a professional engineer
No prior conversational-AI-agent design at enterprise scale (thousands of conversations/day)
In-person San Francisco-primary culture; London office exists but intensity/in-person expectation high
preferred_met: Data-driven strategic insight: data analytics background (Amazon Alexa failure analysis, GA)
Trusted-advisor / customer-facing orientation from founder-facing GTM work

required_skills.calculation: 40 * (5/5) = 40 (communication, stakeholder coordination, execution, product strategy, agent exposure all met)

experience_fit.calculation: 8 yrs generalist fits; enterprise agent-development depth partial relative to role expectations

industry_match.calculation: Agentic AI (top-choice) - Sierra is a leading AI agent platform (Bret Taylor / Clay Bavor)

location_match.calculation: London office; Ryan open to London in-person

notes: Sierra: enterprise AI agent platform co-founded by Bret Taylor (OpenAI board chair, ex-Salesforce co-CEO) and Clay Bavor (ex-Google Labs). Agent Strategist scopes/builds/ships AI agents with PMs and engineers - agentic AI = Ryan's top-choice. London office, in-person.

must_haves.req: Strong understanding of software development, product management AND engineering principles
must_haves.note: Ryan builds agents at operator level (Claude Code) but has no formal SWE or PM-title background. The listing wants genuine software-dev + PM + eng grounding to act as strategic technical partner.

must_haves.req: Outstanding communication conveying complex technical info to non-technical stakeholders
Drive execution of complex, high-visibility multi-project delivery
must_haves.note: Ran presale, ambassador programme, node-runner network end-to-end.

must_haves.req: Coordinate technical + non-technical stakeholders across full agent-dev lifecycle
Leadership/team management; manage multiple projects, lead teams
must_haves.note: Programme/functional leadership, but no documented people-management headcount or formal team-lead role.

must_haves.req: Degree in CS/Eng/Maths or equivalent (strong plus); MBA (strong plus)
must_haves.note: Listed as 'strong plus' not hard-required; Ryan has non-technical BA. Equivalent practical experience is allowed but his is operator-level, not technical-depth.

hard_blockers: Bar for 'strong understanding of software development AND engineering principles' implies real technical/PM depth to partner with Agent Engineers; Ryan's agent-building is operator-level, not professional software engineering.
Building conversational agents handling thousands of conversations/day at enterprise scale — no prior enterprise agent-development track record.
met_strengths: Direct, current AI-agent-building experience (Claude Code) — genuinely on-point for an agent-development role
London office; in-person/intensity culture acceptable; UK work auth fine
interview_match.one_line_verdict: His agent-building and communication fit the spirit of the role, but the explicit software-development + PM + engineering-principles bar (degree a 'strong plus', enterprise-scale agent dev expected) makes this an honest stretch rather than a plausible match.
